title: Colombo & Hurd Recognized in Chambers USA 2026
content:
ORLANDO, Fla. (June 4, 2026) — Colombo & Hurd is proud to announce that the firm has been recognized in the 2026 edition of Chambers USA , widely regarded as one of the legal industry’s leading ranking publications.
Chambers and Partners has been ranking the world’s top law firms and lawyers for over 30 years. Chambers rankings are based on extensive independent research, including interviews with clients, in-house counsel and industry peers evaluating legal knowledge, client service, responsiveness, commercial awareness and professional conduct.
“We are proud to be recognized by Chambers USA for our immigration practice,” said Rusten Hurd, co-founder of Colombo & Hurd. “This ranking is a testament to the hard work of our attorneys and their commitment to delivering exceptional results for our clients.”
About Colombo & Hurd
Founded in 2006, Colombo & Hurd is a U.S. immigration law firm representing professionals, entrepreneurs, and investors from over 100 countries. With a global team of over 400, the firm brings two decades of focused practice across high-skilled and investor immigration. Since 2023 alone, the firm has secured more than 3,000 immigration approvals for high-skilled professionals, entrepreneurs, investors, and their families.
What distinguishes the practice is the depth of its experience and the breadth of expertise across the team. Alongside accomplished immigration attorneys, the firm draws on the perspective of a former USCIS adjudicator and a federal litigation practice led by a former U.S. Department of Justice litigator, giving it a nuanced understanding of how cases are reviewed, decided, and, where necessary, challenged in court. That range allows the firm to handle matters from initial strategy through federal litigation against improper denials and unreasonable delays.
The firm has also earned the AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell, the highest distinction for legal ability and ethical standards, for each of the last 18 years. The firm’s mission is to empower the world’s brightest professionals and entrepreneurs to achieve their immigration dreams in the United States.
